attacking_style: Thrives on extremely steep gradients (30%+ slopes), capable of holding position against elite chasers in late-race scenarios from breakaway positions.
team_tactics: Operates from breakaway positions rather than relying on team-led setups, suggesting autonomy in stage hunting tactics.
terrain_preference: Excels on brutally steep, punchy climbs where gradient exceeds 20-30%. Suits mountain stages with explosive, high-gradient finales rather than sustained tempo climbs.
vulnerability: Limited data prevents clear identification, but breakaway reliance suggests potential weakness in direct GC contender confrontations on long, steady climbs.
key_patterns: Single race observation: won from breakaway on 30% gradient against Contador's chase
Demonstrated strong climbing form in Grand Tour late-stage mountain finishes
Capable of resisting pressure from elite climbers on extreme gradients
form_trajectory: positive: showed strong climbing form with breakthrough win on extreme gradient, holding off elite opposition
